{"problem_standardized_math":"Let $(x,s,y,z)$ be a primal-dual solution satisfying the LICQ and strict complementarity conditions. Show that, as $\\mu \\to 0$, some diagonal elements of $\\Sigma$ tend to infinity while others tend to zero, and characterize which case occurs for each diagonal element. Consider both definitions\n\\[\n\\Sigma=S^{-1}Z \\qquad \\text{(19.13)}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\Sigma=\\mu S^{-2} \\qquad \\text{(19.14)}.\n\\]"}
